title: Feeding 6 Well Plates
author: Andrea Argouarch
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8gihtue

steps.instruction: Record observations Biopsy pieces should look dense and dark; it will not change morphology Clean and straight edges on the tissue is best Outgrowth will vary between a few days to 2 weeks, depending on biopsy After a week, gently change media and then feed every 2-3 days o Be careful not to move coverslip and to not tilt plate
Turn off UV lights and clean hood with 70% ethanol

Clean items with 70% ethanol and bring into hood a. Sterile Filtered Media with PenStrep DMEM, high glucose, pyruvate Thermo Fisher Catalog #11995073 Penicillin-Streptomycin Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #15140122 Fetal Bovine Serum VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #97068-091
Carefully remove old media with vacuum line, making sure not to touch the coverslip.
Do not tilt plate while feeding to prevent coverslip from sliding
Add 3 mls 3 mL of new media per well, making sure not to touch the coverslip a. Add media slowly b. Make sure the coverslip is still firmly and evenly over the biopsy
Gently place 6 well plate in incubator (5% CO2, 37 o C) 37 °C
Continue to gently feed every 2 – 3 days for 3 weeks to allow enough cells to emerge and expand from tissue
Clean and sterilize hood with 70% ethanol and turn on UV
Update cell culture notes in lab notebook
